title: Temperature Control Algorithm
description: Simple DDC temperature control loop showing how BMS maintains setpoint
code:
// BMS Temperature Control Loop
BEGIN
 SET desiredTemp = 22 // Setpoint in Celsius
 SET tolerance = 0.5
 
 WHILE systemActive DO
 currentTemp = READ_TEMPERATURE_SENSOR(sensorID)
 
 IF currentTemp > (desiredTemp + tolerance) THEN
 // Room too hot - Cooling required
 IF coolingSystem = OFF THEN
 WRITE_OUTPUT(coolingCoil, ON)
 WRITE_OUTPUT(heatingCoil, OFF)
 END IF
 ELSE IF currentTemp < (desiredTemp - tolerance) THEN
 // Room too cold - Heating required
 IF heatingSystem = OFF THEN
 WRITE_OUTPUT(heatingCoil, ON)
 WRITE_OUTPUT(coolingCoil, OFF)
 END IF
 ELSE
 // Within tolerance - System stable
 WRITE_OUTPUT(coolingCoil, OFF)
 WRITE_OUTPUT(heatingCoil, OFF)
 END IF
 
 LOG_TREND(currentTemp, timestamp)
 WAIT(samplingInterval)
 END WHILE
END
explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates basic DDC temperature control where the controller compares actual temperature to setpoint and commands heating/cooling actuators accordingly. It includes trend logging for historical analysis.

title: Occupancy-Based Lighting Control
description: Lighting control algorithm using occupancy sensors to save energy
code:
// Occupancy-Based Lighting Control
CONSTANTS
 ON_TIME = 900 // 15 minutes delay before turning off
 DIM_LEVEL = 30 // Dim to 30% when occupied
 FULL_LEVEL = 100 // Full brightness

VARIABLES
 lastOccupancyTime
 isOccupied
 currentLightLevel

BEGIN
 WHILE systemActive DO
 isOccupied = READ_OCCUPANCY_SENSOR(sensorID)
 
 IF isOccupied THEN
 lastOccupancyTime = CURRENT_TIME()
 currentLightLevel = FULL_LEVEL
 WRITE_OUTPUT(lightingCircuit, FULL_LEVEL)
 LOG_EVENT("Occupancy detected - Lights ON")
 ELSE
 // Check if occupied time exceeded delay
 IF (CURRENT_TIME() - lastOccupancyTime) > ON_TIME THEN
 currentLightLevel = 0
 WRITE_OUTPUT(lightingCircuit, OFF)
 LOG_EVENT("No occupancy - Lights OFF")
 ELSE
 // During delay period - maintain current
 currentLightLevel = currentLightLevel
 END IF
 END IF
 
 // Optional: Daylight harvesting integration
 ambientLight = READ_LIGHT_SENSOR(ambientSensor)
 IF isOccupied AND ambientLight < MIN_AMBIENT THEN
 dimAmount = calculateDimming(ambientLight)
 WRITE_OUTPUT(lightingCircuit, FULL_LEVEL - dimAmount)
 END IF
 
 WAIT(samplingInterval)
 END WHILE
END
explanation: This algorithm saves energy by turning lights off after a configurable delay when no occupancy is detected. It also integrates daylight harvesting to reduce artificial lighting when natural light is sufficient.

title: Demand Response Algorithm
description: BMS demand response logic to reduce load during peak demand events
code:
// Demand Response Controller
CONSTANTS
 MAX_DEMAND_THRESHOLD = 500 // kW
 HIGH_DEMAND_THRESHOLD = 450 // kW
 NORMAL_THRESHOLD = 400 // kW

VARIABLES
 currentDemand
 demandResponseActive
 loadReductionTarget

BEGIN
 // Listen for utility demand response signal
 ON EVENT(utilitySignal) DO
 demandSignal = PARSE_SIGNAL(event.data)
 
 SWITCH demandSignal.signalType
 CASE "EMERGENCY":
 loadReductionTarget = 0.20 // Reduce 20%
 SET_PRIORITY_LEVEL(emergency)
 CASE "HIGH":
 loadReductionTarget = 0.15 // Reduce 15%
 SET_PRIORITY_LEVEL(high)
 CASE "NORMAL":
 loadReductionTarget = 0
 SET_PRIORITY_LEVEL(normal)
 demandResponseActive = FALSE
 END SWITCH
 
 IF loadReductionTarget > 0 THEN
 demandResponseActive = TRUE
 EXECUTE_LOAD_SHEDDING(loadReductionTarget)
 LOG_EVENT("Demand Response Activated: " + loadReductionTarget)
 END IF
 END EVENT
END

PROCEDURE EXECUTE_LOAD_SHEDDING(target)
 // Priority: Non-critical loads first
 priority1 = GET_LOADS_BY_PRIORITY(1) // Lighting
 priority2 = GET_LOADS_BY_PRIORITY(2) // HVAC pre-cooling
 priority3 = GET_LOADS_BY_PRIORITY(3) // Equipment
 
 totalReduction = 0
 FOR EACH load IN priority1 DO
 IF totalReduction < target THEN
 reduction = ADJUST_LOAD(load, -0.30)
 totalReduction = totalReduction + reduction
 END IF
 END FOR
 // Continue with priority 2 and 3... 
 LOG_EVENT("Load Reduction Achieved: " + totalReduction)
END PROCEDURE
explanation: This demand response algorithm responds to utility signals by automatically shedding non-critical loads during peak demand periods. It prioritizes loads to maintain comfort while achieving required reduction targets.

complexity.time: O(n) where n = number of controllable loads

title: BMS Scheduling Algorithm
description: Time-based scheduling for HVAC and lighting systems based on building occupancy patterns
code:
// BMS Scheduling Algorithm
DATA
 schedule[] = {
 {day: "MONDAY", zone: "OFFICE", start: "08:00", end: "18:00", mode: "OCCUPIED"},
 {day: "MONDAY", zone: "OFFICE", start: "18:00", end: "08:00", mode: "UNOCCUPIED"},
 {day: "FRIDAY", zone: "OFFICE", start: "08:00", end: "17:00", mode: "OCCUPIED"}
 }

VARIABLES
 currentTime
 currentZone
 currentMode

BEGIN
 WHILE systemActive DO
 currentTime = GET_CURRENT_TIME()
 currentDay = GET_CURRENT_DAY()
 
 FOR EACH zone IN buildingZones DO
 activeSchedule = FIND_SCHEDULE(currentDay, zone)
 
 FOR EACH period IN activeSchedule DO
 IF (currentTime >= period.start) AND (currentTime < period.end) THEN
 currentMode = period.mode
 BREAK
 ELSE
 currentMode = "UNOCCUPIED"
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Apply mode-specific settings
 APPLY_ZONE_SETTINGS(zone, currentMode)
 END FOR
 
 WAIT(60) // Check every minute
 END WHILE
END

PROCEDURE APPLY_ZONE_SETTINGS(zone, mode)
 SWITCH mode
 CASE "OCCUPIED":
 SET_HVAC_SETPOINT(zone, heating=20, cooling=24)
 SET_LIGHTING(zone, ON, 100%)
 SET_VENTILATION(zone, FULL_AIR)
 CASE "UNOCCUPIED":
 SET_HVAC_SETPOINT(zone, heating=15, cooling=28) // Night setback
 SET_LIGHTING(zone, OFF, 0%)
 SET_VENTILATION(zone, MINIMUM_AIR)
 CASE "OVERNIGHT":
 SET_HVAC_SETPOINT(zone, heating=12, cooling=30)
 SET_LIGHTING(zone, EMERGENCY, 10%)
 SET_VENTILATION(zone, OFF)
 END SWITCH
END PROCEDURE
explanation: This scheduling algorithm ensures HVAC and lighting operate according to predefined occupancy patterns, applying night setback during unoccupied periods for significant energy savings.
